title: Early growth observation for 3 Sisters Plus planting
description: Initial field observation of germination, soil moisture, companion emergence, and early pest pressure in the Spring 2026 3 Sisters Plus trial at the Veneta stewardship site.

conditions.notes: Hugelkultur bed appears to be retaining moisture well despite several dry intervals between rains.

species_observed.notes: Several corn seedlings visible, though emergence is uneven across the bed.
Light clover emergence visible in patches.
Volunteer or companion mustard seedlings present at bed margins.

biotic_interactions.notes: Slug activity evident on several emerging seedlings.
A few native bees observed nearby, though flowering is still limited.

soil_and_ecology_notes: Surface mulch is moderating moisture loss. Soil structure appears to be loosening compared with earlier preparation stages. Early fungal activity is suspected beneath woody hugelkultur layers, though not directly measured.
unexpected_findings: Mustard emergence may be contributing unplanned crucifer presence in the polyculture.
Corn emergence is patchier than expected, possibly reflecting micro-variation in moisture or seed depth.

recommended_next_actions: continue monitoring bean and squash emergence
record soil moisture changes after next dry interval

metadata.notes: Initial observation example for the Veneta 3 Sisters Plus planting.
